A Brand Identity Concept — Ruby Hue Artisan Chocolates

Bean-to-bar craft deserves
a brand to match.

Artisan Chocolate Makers · Bristol

The Logomark

Ruby Hue — Four Expressions

Primary — Dark Ground
Reversed — Light Ground
Heritage — Chocolate Ground
Signature — Ruby Ground
The Opportunity

The craft is extraordinary.
The brand should say so.

Ruby Hue has everything a premium brand needs at its core — a genuinely rare skill in bean-to-bar chocolate making, a compelling founding story, and a product that customers describe as unforgettable. What it does not yet have is a visual identity that commands the same level of respect. That gap is exactly where Brand Architect works.

The mark has character the brand has not yet claimed

The logomark carries warmth, craft, and distinction. But a mark only reaches its full potential when it sits within a system that pulls everything — colour, type, tone, layout — in the same direction. That system does not yet exist.

The digital presence does not reflect the in-person experience

Customers walk into Ruby Hue and describe it as a revelation. The website does not create that feeling. A brand at this level should make someone feel something before they have even placed an order.

Typography is not doing its job

The right typefaces signal standing before a single word is read. In the fine chocolate market, that signal is the difference between a product that feels artisan and one that feels premium. Right now, Ruby Hue is not making that distinction.

The founding story is an untapped asset

A fine dining chef and an award-winning cocktail bartender obsessively crafting bean-to-bar chocolate in Bristol — that is not a backstory, it is a brand. The credentials of Ru and Tom are among the most powerful things Ruby Hue owns. They deserve a much bigger stage.

There is no unified visual voice across touchpoints

Packaging, digital, and social all speak in slightly different registers. Premium brands are consistent at every point of contact — not because it looks tidy, but because consistency is what builds the kind of trust that turns first-time buyers into loyal customers.

The price point is not being supported by the identity

Ruby Hue charges a premium — and the product absolutely justifies it. But brand identity is what makes a customer feel confident paying that price before they have even tasted the chocolate. That confidence is being left to chance.

Proposed Palette

Colour that carries character.

Colour is not decoration — it is identity. A considered palette applied with consistency becomes as recognisable as the mark itself.

Deep Dark #1A0A0A
Chocolate #3D1A0A
Ruby #8B1A1A
Blush #D4A090
Parchment #F2F2F2
Typography

Typography that earns its place.

The right typefaces do not simply present words — they signal standing. In the specialty food and drink market, typography is one of the clearest indicators of whether a brand belongs at the premium tier. It is a detail customers feel before they consciously notice it.

Display Serif — crafted
for distinction
From bean to bar, by hand.
Subheading Serif — authority
without weight
Fine cacao. Ethically sourced. Crafted in Bristol.
Body Sans — clean,
considered, modern
Ru and Tom source, roast, crack, winnow, and stone grind every batch themselves. No shortcuts. No compromise. Every bar tells the story of the farm it came from.
Label / UI Sans — precision
at every scale
70% Dark · Single Origin · Solomon Islands
The Opportunity

"The craft inside Ruby Hue is exceptional. The opportunity is to build a brand around it that is equally impossible to ignore."

— Brand Architect
Next Step

This is only the beginning, Ru & Tom.

What you see here is a speculative glimpse — a direction, not a destination. The full Brand Architect identity package encompasses twelve artboards: brand heart, primary logomark, all variations, colour and typography systems, digital and physical applications. Everything Ruby Hue needs to carry the weight of the chocolate it makes.
